title: Aloo Paratha

overview.description: Wholemeal flatbreads stuffed with a lightly spiced potato filling and cooked on a hot tawa until crisp and golden.

method: Mix the atta and salt.
Add the water and oil and knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth.
Cover and rest for at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, boil the potatoes until tender.
Drain well and allow excess moisture to evaporate.
Mash until completely smooth with no lumps.
Mix in the chilli, coriander, cumin seeds, ground coriander, Kashmiri chilli, garam masala, amchur and salt.
Allow the filling to cool completely.
Divide both the dough and filling into 8 equal portions.
Roll one piece of dough into a circle approximately 12cm across.
Place one portion of filling in the centre.
Bring the edges together over the filling like a money bag.
Twist and pinch the top firmly closed.
Remove any excess dough from the top if necessary.
Flatten gently with your palm.
Allow to rest for 5 minutes.
Dust lightly with atta.
Roll gently from the centre outwards, turning the dough a quarter turn after each roll.
If the filling begins to push through, dust with a little extra atta and continue rolling gently.
Heat a tawa or heavy frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Cook for about 2 minutes until brown spots appear.
Turn, brush lightly with ghee and cook for another 2 minutes.
Flip once more, brush the second side with ghee and cook until both sides are golden with darker toasted spots.

learning.did-you-know: Aloo paratha is one of the most popular breakfasts across North India, particularly in Punjab.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make soft chapati dough, prepare a smooth potato filling and seal and roll stuffed flatbreads without tearing.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A filling breakfast or light meal made almost entirely from pantry staples that are always available.
chapman-tips: The potato filling must be completely smooth or it will tear the dough.
Allowing the stuffed dough to rest before rolling makes it much easier to handle.
Roll gently rather than pressing hard with the rolling pin.
Cook over medium-high heat so the dough cooks through before the outside burns.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze cooked parathas between sheets of baking paper. Reheat directly from frozen in a dry frying pan.
notes.leftovers: Excellent toasted the next day or packed into lunchboxes.
